title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the taste of a traditional Chinese bakery with this homemade Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao recipe!

description: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao are iconic Cantonese sweet buns, famous for their unique crispy, crumbly, and sweet topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Beneath this delightful crust lies a soft, fluffy, and slightly sweet bread, making them a beloved treat in Hong Kong bakeries and beyond. This recipe brings the authentic taste and texture of these beloved buns straight to your kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 250g bread flour, 40g granulated sugar, 3g instant dry yeast, and 2g salt. Add 120ml warm whole milk and 1 whole large egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface or in a stand mixer for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic. Gradually add 30g softened unsalted butter and continue kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is supple.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, cream together 60g cold cubed unsalted butter and 50g powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 large egg yolk and 1 tablespoon evaporated milk until well combined. Sift in 100g all-purpose flour and 1/2 teaspoon baking powder. Mix gently until just combined to form a soft, cookie-like dough. Be careful not to overmix.

instructions.title: Divide and Shape Dough
instructions.text: Divide the prepared bun dough into 8 equal portions, approximately 60-65g each. Roll each portion into a smooth, round ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, ensuring there is enough space between each bun for expansion.

instructions.title: Apply Topping
instructions.text: Divide the pineapple topping dough into 8 equal portions. Take one portion and flatten it into a thin disc, about 3-4 inches in diameter, between two sheets of plastic wrap or by hand. Gently peel off the plastic and place the topping disc over each bun dough ball, pressing lightly to adhere and cover about two-thirds of the bun's surface. You can use a knife to score a criss-cross pattern on the topping for the classic pineapple look.

instructions.title: Final Proofing
instructions.text: Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel. Let them proof in a warm place for about 10 minutes, allowing them to slightly puff up. During this time, preheat your oven to 180 degrees Celsius or 350 degrees Fahrenheit.

instructions.title: Egg Wash and Bake
instructions.text: Gently brush the exposed dough edges of each bun with the beaten egg wash for a golden finish. Bake the buns in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. The internal temperature of the bread should reach 90 degrees Celsius or 195 degrees Fahrenheit.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the Pineapple Buns from the oven and transfer them immediately to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm for the best texture and flavor. These buns are delicious on their own or traditionally enjoyed with a slice of butter inserted into the warm bun.

tags: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe
tips: Do not overmix the topping dough; a crumbly, cookie-like texture is desired for the iconic crispness. Overmixing can make it tough.
For a more pronounced pineapple pattern, use a pastry cutter or the back of a knife to score the topping deeply before baking.
Pineapple buns are best enjoyed fresh and warm. If storing, reheat them briefly in a toaster oven to restore their crispy topping.
article:
Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao, a beloved staple of Hong Kong bakeries and cha chaan tengs, holds a special place in the hearts of those who appreciate the delicate artistry of Chinese baking. Despite its name, this iconic bun contains no actual pineapple. Instead, its distinctive moniker comes from the golden-brown, cracked, and crumbly sugar-cookie-like crust that forms a beautiful pattern resembling the skin of a pineapple. Beneath this sweet, slightly crisp exterior lies an incredibly soft, fluffy, and tender milk bread, creating a delightful contrast in every bite.

If you've ever yearned to recreate that authentic taste and texture in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao can be a rewarding experience, transforming simple ingredients into a comforting masterpiece. Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that will fill your home with the irresistible aroma of freshly baked goods and transport you straight to the bustling streets of Hong Kong. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every step, ensuring your homemade buns are nothing short of spectacular. If you've been searching for the ultimate Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe, your quest ends here.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

The Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao (菠蘿包), is more than just a pastry; it's a cultural icon, a symbol of Hong Kong's vibrant food scene. Its magic lies in the exquisite balance between its two main components: a rich, tender, slightly sweet bread dough and a sugary, buttery, crumbly topping. The bread itself is often made with a milk-enriched dough, similar to Japanese shokupan, giving it an unparalleled softness. The topping, a simple mix of flour, sugar, butter, and egg, is spread thinly over the proofed dough before baking. As it bakes, the topping expands and cracks, creating the characteristic "pineapple" appearance and a texture that is both delicate and satisfyingly crisp.

While traditionally served plain, allowing the bread and topping to shine, variations with sweet or savory fillings have become popular. However, the most classic way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun is fresh out of the oven, perhaps split open and generously slathered with a thick slab of cold butter (known as Bo Lo Yau). Understanding the components of this Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is key to appreciating its unique charm and mastering its creation.

## Ingredients You Need

To create your own batch of delicious Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao, you'll need two sets of ingredients: one for the milk bread dough and one for the signature pineapple topping. Gathering these ingredients is the first step to mastering this authentic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe.

For the Milk Bread Dough:
*   3 cups (360g) bread flour
*   1/4 cup (50g) granulated sugar
*   1 teaspoon (5g) salt
*   2 teaspoons (6g) instant dry yeast
*   1 large egg, room temperature
*   3/4 cup (180ml) warm milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C)
*   1/4 cup (56g) unsalted butter, softened

For the Pineapple Topping:
*   1/2 cup (113g) unsalted butter, softened
*   1/2 cup (100g) granulated sugar
*   1 large egg yolk
*   1 1/2 cups (180g) all-purpose flour
*   1 tablespoon milk powder (optional, for richer flavor)
*   1/2 teaspoon baking powder
*   Pinch of salt
*   1-2 tablespoons milk, if needed for consistency

For Egg Wash:
*   1 large egg, beaten

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow this detailed Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe carefully for the best results.

1.  **Prepare the Milk Bread Dough:** In a large bowl, whisk together the bread flour, sugar, salt, and instant yeast. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg and warm milk. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
2.  **Knead the Dough:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for about 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened butter a little at a time, kneading until fully absorbed and the dough becomes very smooth, pliable, and passes the windowpane test (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
3.  **First Rise:** Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
4.  **Prepare the Pineapple Topping:** While the dough is rising, cream the softened butter and sugar together in a medium bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk. In a separate bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, milk powder (if using), baking powder, and salt. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined and a soft dough forms. If the mixture is too dry, add milk a teaspoon at a time until it comes together. Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
5.  **Shape the Buns:** Once the bread dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 8-12 equal portions (depending on desired bun size, typically 60-80g each). Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place the dough balls on a parchment-lined baking sheet, leaving space between them.
6.  **Form the Topping and Assemble:** Remove the chilled topping dough from the refrigerator. Divide it into the same number of portions as your bread dough. On a lightly floured surface, flatten each portion of topping dough into a thin disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter). Carefully drape a topping disc over each bread dough ball, gently pressing down to adhere it around the sides, leaving the bottom exposed. Use a knife to lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping – this creates the "pineapple" effect.
7.  **Second Rise:** Cover the buns loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.
8.  **Bake:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly brush the scored topping of each bun with the beaten egg wash. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the bread is cooked through.
9.  **Cool:** Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving bakery-quality Pineapple Buns requires a little attention to detail. These tips will elevate your Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe from good to outstanding:

*   **Don't Rush the Kneading:** Proper kneading is crucial for developing gluten, which gives the bread its signature softness and chewiness.
*   **Proofing Environment:** A warm, slightly humid environment (like a turned-off oven with a bowl of hot water inside) is ideal for consistent rising.
*   **Topping Consistency:** The topping dough should be soft enough to spread but firm enough to handle. If it's too sticky, add a little more flour; if too crumbly, add a tiny bit more milk or egg yolk. Chilling it makes it much easier to work with.
*   **Gentle Handling:** Handle both the bread dough and topping gently to maintain their structure and air pockets.
*   **Scoring:** Use a sharp knife and score only the topping, not too deep into the bread dough, to achieve the classic pattern.
*   **Even Baking:** Rotate your baking sheet halfway through baking for uniform browning.

## Serving Suggestions

Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven, when the contrast between the crispy topping and soft bread is at its peak.

*   **Classic Bo Lo Yau:** Slice horizontally and insert a thick, cold slab of butter for a truly indulgent experience. The butter melts into the warm bread, creating a sublime richness.
*   **With Beverages:** Pair with a traditional Hong Kong milk tea, strong coffee, or simply a glass of milk for a comforting breakfast or afternoon treat.
*   **Plain and Simple:** Enjoy them as they are, appreciating the subtle sweetness and textures.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is delicious on its own, don't hesitate to experiment:

*   **Fillings:** Common fillings include sweet custard cream, red bean paste, coconut, or even savory char siu (BBQ pork). Simply flatten the dough ball, place a spoonful of filling in the center, and seal it before adding the topping.
*   **Topping Flavor:** Add a pinch of cocoa powder to the topping for a chocolate twist, or a hint of almond extract for a different aroma.
*   **Dough Enhancements:** For an even richer dough, consider using a tangzhong (water roux) method, which involves cooking a portion of the flour and liquid before adding it to the main dough.
*   **Dairy-Free:** Substitute milk with a plant-based alternative and use a dairy-free butter substitute for both the dough and topping.

With this comprehensive Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe, you're now ready to bring the authentic taste of Hong Kong's most famous bun into your own home. The journey of making these buns is as delightful as eating them. So roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to impress your family and friends with these irresistible golden delights!
